title: Disease burden from non-communicable diseases vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: Disease burden to non-communicable diseases (NCDs), measured in DALYs (Disability-Adjusted Life Years) per 100,000 individuals versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
caption:
Disease burden from non-communicable diseases vs. GDP per capita, 2023

Disease burden to non-communicable diseases (NCDs), measured in DALYs (Disability-Adjusted Life Years) per 100,000 individuals versus gross domestic product (GDP) per capita, adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.

This scatter plot maps the relationship between national wealth and health outcomes for 2023, with GDP per capita expressed in international-$ at 2021 prices plotted on a logarithmic horizontal axis. The vertical axis represents the disease burden from non-communicable diseases in DALYs per 100,000 individuals, ranging from 0 to 50,000. A general downward trend is visible, where countries with lower GDP per capita, particularly those in Africa such as Burundi, Niger, and the DR Congo, cluster on the left side with high disease burdens between 20,000 and 30,000 DALYs. In contrast, high-income nations in North America and Europe, including the United States, Germany, and Japan, are positioned on the far right with GDP per capita exceeding $50,000 and disease burdens generally falling between 10,000 and 25,000 DALYs. Several outliers appear in the upper middle section of the chart; Nauru records the highest burden near 50,000 DALYs, while the Solomon Islands, Marshall Islands, and Haiti show values between 35,000 and 42,000 despite varying income levels. Circle sizes correspond to population, highlighting large nations like India, China, and Indonesia in the middle of the distribution with burdens roughly between 15,000 and 25,000 DALYs. Singapore appears at the extreme right with a GDP per capita approaching $100,000 and a relatively low disease burden near 15,000 DALYs.
